operational_question: Can the record identify what kind of model artifact it is?
main_measure: artifact-role and variant fields; 300-repository expert audit
key_result: project-inferred artifact role available for 100.0%; project-inferred variant type available for 100.0%; strict audited role agreement 85.3%
curation_implication: The collection can be typed at scale, but boundary cases require explicit packaging overlays.

operational_question: Can the record connect artifacts to creators, dates, papers, and external project records?
primary_record_source: Repository metadata and scholarly paper text
main_measure: stable id, owner, creation date, scholarly references, paper-side links
key_result: stable id/owner/date 100.0%/100.0%/100.0%; repository scholarly reference 33.3%; paper HF link 10.5%; paper GitHub link 30.6%
curation_implication: Basic citation handles are strong, but bidirectional paper-repository linkage is incomplete.

operational_question: Can the record support evidence-backed claims about upstream models, families, teachers, or bases?
primary_record_source: Paper-family links, exact paper-HF edges, repository upstream metadata, expert lineage audit
main_measure: paper-level evidence tiers and 200-row expert lineage audit
key_result: paper exact HF edge 5.6%; inventory-backed family signal 45.6%; expert strict support 28.0% of audited rows.; expert usable support 79.0% of audited rows.
curation_implication: Public records are useful for family-level discovery but much weaker for exact genealogy.

operational_question: Can the record tell whether an artifact package is inspectable, reusable, or preservable?
primary_record_source: Scholarly paper release statements and extracted links
key_result: any release/open-asset signal 67.2%; concrete code/asset signal 35.8%; strict full package 4.5%
curation_implication: Open release must be treated as a hierarchy rather than a binary available/not available flag.

operational_question: How much combined evidence do paper records provide for provenance, release packages, and paper-repository linkage?
primary_record_source: Combined paper-level provenance, release, and link signals
main_measure: curatability category derived from independent evidence components
key_result: high curatability 6.1% jointly satisfies provenance, concrete release, and link criteria; high or operational curatability 18.1% under the operational rubric
curation_implication: Many records are useful for discovery, but fewer support strong end-to-end curation workflows without additional metadata.
